name: Infrastructure
description: Build configuration, package management, IDE tooling, and static assets. This layer bootstraps the project — it defines how the app is built, deployed, and developed, but contains no domain logic.

name: Foundation/Core
description: Core types, global state stores, design tokens, and Supabase client instances. This layer is the contract between infrastructure and domain logic — it defines the shared language (types), runtime state container ($state stores), visual vocabulary (CSS custom properties), and data transport (Supabase clients) that every other layer depends on.

name: LLM Provider Router
description: Model-agnostic LLM adapter layer. Contains provider-specific implementations (Ollama, OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic) and a central router that dispatches requests without hardcoding any single model. Configuration is driven entirely by environment variables.

name: JARVIS Engine
description: Core JARVIS business logic. This is the central domain layer containing memory persistence, conversation intelligence, tool registry, skill system, safety policy enforcement, automation scheduler, self-evaluation analytics, and briefing composition engine. All layers above depend on Foundation; all layers below depend on this layer.

name: API/Server Endpoints
description: SvelteKit server-side API routes that expose JARVIS Engine capabilities over HTTP. Each endpoint wraps a JARVIS runtime module with request parsing, error handling, and response serialization. This layer is the network boundary between client UI and server logic.

name: UI/Frontend
description: SvelteKit client-side pages and layouts. Each page is a Svelte component that consumes API endpoints and JARVIS runtime modules to render interactive views for the user's workspace.

name: Agent/Skill System
description: Claude Code agent definitions, skill specifications, hook scripts, and session protocol files. This layer configures the AI coding assistant that operates this repository — it is external to the running application but governs how Claude interacts with it during development.

name: Data/Storage
description: Database schema migrations and local workspace state. Supabase provides the persistent SQL layer; workspace.json holds runtime configuration on disk.
